summary:
The correct answer relates to the initiation of school dental nursing programs in specific English regions such as New Cross during early 20th century public health reforms aimed at improving children’s oral hygiene through accessible preventative services. This reflects an important shift toward integrating dental healthcare into educational settings for better community-wide outcomes.

The agent identifies that while no direct evidence exists about starting school dental nurses globally or universally in 1921, there were localized implementations—specifically referencing “New Cross” as part of broader UK public health strategies introducing school-based dental care around that time. These local pilot projects laid foundational steps towards systemic integration of pediatric dentistry within education systems by focusing prevention rather than treatment.
